finding: A prompt explicitly asking to continue repairing all backlog items was routed by `next --prompt`/`guide` to the generic `create-atom` route, requiring bucket/title/description/logical-name evidence instead of selecting the existing backlog/task queue. This can send Captain continuation work into an unrelated atom-birth flow.
expectedBehavior: Backlog-repair and Captain-continuation language should resolve to the existing governed backlog queue or a task-selection route; generic create-atom fallback must not be selected when the prompt names backlog repair and ongoing continuation.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced on 2026-07-12 after TASK-AAO-0177 close: `node atm.mjs next --prompt "請繼續修復所有 backlog；過程中所有不好用或卡住的地方都回寫 backlog。" --json` returned `prompt-guidance-required`, and `node atm.mjs start --goal ...` selected `recommendedRoute: create-atom` with missing bucket/title/description/logical-name evidence. TASK-AAO-FABLE-004 extends queue-request prompt normalization for zh-TW backlog continuation, English `continue backlog fixes`, and Captain-mode backlog repairs, with `validate:prompt-scoped-next` fixtures asserting batch queue routing instead of prompt-guidance/create-atom fallback.
followUp: Keep follow-up row 173 for task-id alias ergonomics; core backlog continuation queue routing is fixed in TASK-AAO-FABLE-004.
